description: Questbook UI polish: animated flash overlay (slide-in/out from top), animated pressure chip color transitions, and glitchy false quest markers
steps: In composeApp/src/commonMain/kotlin/ui/rpg/SliceScreen.kt, rewrite SliceQuestbookFlash to use AnimatedVisibility with slideInVertically (from top) + fadeIn for enter, slideOutVertically (to top) + fadeOut for exit. The composable currently just appears/disappears. Wrap it in AnimatedVisibility keyed on the flashText state being non-null.

In the same file, rewrite SlicePressureChip to use animateColorAsState for the background color so that when pressure changes from LOW->MEDIUM->HIGH, the color smoothly transitions (green -> amber -> red) with a visible animation (use tween or spring spec).

Add a new composable SliceFalseMarkerChip (or similar) that renders false quest markers with a glitchy visual effect: slight random offset (translationX jittering via infinite animation), and a flicker effect (alpha oscillating between 0.5 and 1.0 on a fast cycle). Use rememberInfiniteTransition for the flicker/jitter.

Integrate SliceFalseMarkerChip into the ExploreView by displaying director.falseMarkers when they exist, positioned distinctly from real quest markers. Show them as small chips with the glitchy animation below the pressure chip.
Add compose.animation import to the build.gradle.kts commonMain dependencies if needed (it may already be included via compose.foundation).
acceptance_criteria: SliceQuestbookFlash uses AnimatedVisibility with slide-in from top and slide-out to top
SlicePressureChip color animates smoothly on pressure changes using animateColorAsState
False quest markers display with visible glitch effect (jitter + flicker animation)
No LLM/AI calls - everything is deterministic Compose animation
verification: Run ./gradlew desktopTest and confirm all existing tests pass
Run ./gradlew build to ensure compilation succeeds
findings: All animation APIs (AnimatedVisibility, animateColorAsState, rememberInfiniteTransition, graphicsLayer) are available from compose.foundation without additional dependencies. Build and all tests pass successfully. The unused import for alpha modifier was avoided by using graphicsLayer for the alpha effect in SliceFalseMarkerChip.
